chapters.title: The Choosing
chapters.scaffold: The Emberbearers are summoned to the Hearthstone by Elder Maren. The stone flickers — something it has never done in living memory. Maren explains the Emberlight is weakening and that the Hollow grows bolder by the day. She reveals Embershards, hidden for generations, and tells the young heroes they have been chosen. Each takes a shard, feels its warmth, and chooses their class. Maren warns them: the shards grow only through effort, learning, and cooperation. There are no shortcuts. Rook appears, offers first supplies. Professor Ignis assigns first lessons. The adventure begins.
chapters.tone: Wonder and weight. The beginning of something enormous.

player_count_notes.single: Maren remarks that a lone Emberbearer has not been seen in centuries. The weight is immense, but so is the potential. NPCs rally around the single hero with extra support.
player_count_notes.pair: Maren notes that two bearers in one family is extraordinary — they will need each other.
player_count_notes.group: Maren is astonished. This many Emberbearers from one household has never occurred. The Hearthstone must sense a great threat coming to call so many at once.

chapters.title: The Blighted Clearing
chapters.scaffold: Reports come in of a grove near Hearthhold turning gray. The Emberbearers investigate and find Thornmaw — a corrupted oak. Its roots spread sickness through the soil. Elder Maren explains that the only way to weaken the creature is to strengthen the Emberlight through their deeds. The heroes begin their first quests. The chapter ends with them facing Thornmaw's grove, shards glowing, ready to begin.
chapters.tone: First real danger. Determination mixed with nervousness.

chapters.title: Root and Resolve
chapters.scaffold: Dynamic chapter based on player progress. If boss HP is low: the heroes have fought hard and Thornmaw's bark cracks, revealing green wood. If boss HP is high: Thornmaw resists, and they must dig deeper. References the actual chores and challenges completed. Kaya appears for the first time, scouting the perimeter — impressed but tries not to show it.
must_include: each player gets a named moment
chapters.tone: Action and camaraderie. Learning to work together.

chapters.title: Voices in the Ash
chapters.scaffold: With Thornmaw defeated (or carried over), a new threat emerges: a swarm of ashen moths that whisper discouragement. Villagers begin losing motivation. Professor Ignis recognizes the Whispering Swarm from old texts and urges the heroes to study — knowledge is the swarm's weakness. The Academy becomes critical this week.
chapters.tone: Creeping unease. The enemy fights with words, not claws.

chapters.title: The Immovable
chapters.scaffold: Grulk appears on the main path out of Hearthhold. He doesn't attack. He simply stands there. No one can get past. Supplies can't come in. Traders can't leave. Elder Maren says Grulk represents stagnation itself — he can only be eroded through steady, persistent work. No single blow will fell him.
chapters.tone: Frustrating but not hopeless. The lesson of daily discipline.

chapters.title: The Light That Lies
chapters.scaffold: A mysterious figure appears on the road, glowing with what seems like Emberlight. She calls herself Cindra and offers to help — shortcuts, easy power, a faster way to restore the Hearthstone. Some villagers are tempted. But the Embershards feel cold near her. Something is wrong.
chapters.tone: Suspense. First moral complexity. Things aren't always what they seem.

chapters.title: Unmasked
chapters.scaffold: The Emberbearers expose Cindra. Her false flame gutters and dies. Behind where she stood: an ancient Emberlight shrine, the first of many scattered across Embervale. The Chronicler appears for the first time, writing everything down in light. They say: 'The first arc is complete. But the world is wide, and the Hollow is patient.' A message arrives from the Shattered Coast. Someone needs help.
chapters.tone: Triumph and expansion. The world just got bigger.

chapters.title: The Gray Tide
chapters.scaffold: A battered messenger from Driftmere arrives at Hearthhold: the sea has turned gray, fish are washing up dead, and a ghostly ship has been seen raiding coastal lanterns. The Chronicler appears at the Hearthstone, scratching warnings into light. Elder Maren tells the Emberbearers that the Emberlight must now extend beyond the forest — to the coast. Captain Murk's ship sails on a wind that doesn't move the air, only steals light.
must_include: Driftmere described as a struggling fishing village
chapters.tone: New frontier unease. The Emberbearers leave familiar ground for the first time.

chapters.title: Shadows on the Water
chapters.scaffold: The Emberbearers arrive at Driftmere and see the full scope of the crisis. Captain Murk's raiders have been stealing not just lantern-light but the villagers' will to carry on. Every chore done and lesson learned pushes back the fog clinging to the coast. The chapter reflects the household's activity level: active households see Murk's ship breaking apart; slower weeks show the crew growing bolder.
must_include: each player gets a named moment
chapters.tone: Nautical dread mixed with coastal resilience. The smell of salt and ash.

chapters.title: The Deep Hunger
chapters.scaffold: With Murk driven back, something worse rises from the depths: the Abyssal Maw, a creature more mouth than body. The water around Driftmere turns to sludge. Fishing is impossible. Professor Ignis arrives via a hastily-constructed coastal study and identifies the creature as a Hollow predator drawn to the weakening of the Emberlight under the waves. The Academy's lessons about marine ecosystems become critical.
must_include: Abyssal Maw description as a bottomless hunger
emphasis on educational challenges this week
the water turning to gray sludge
chapters.tone: Creeping horror from below. The enemy is vast, ancient, and hungry.

chapters.title: The Still Tide
chapters.scaffold: The sea grows eerily calm. No waves. No wind. A song drifts across the water — beautiful and empty. Villagers stop working and stare at the horizon, forgetting their chores. The Siren of the Still Tide has arrived. Elder Maren sends word that the only defense is purposeful action. Those who keep busy, keep themselves. Those who stop and listen... don't start again.
must_include: the unnatural stillness of the sea
chapters.tone: Haunting beauty. The danger of rest without purpose.

chapters.title: Breaking the Silence
chapters.scaffold: The Emberbearers fight the Siren's influence through their own activity. Every chore done is a voice raised against the silence. Every lesson learned is a clear note in the counter-melody. Active households see the Siren's song faltering; quieter weeks show the sea growing stiller. Kaya scouts the coastline and reports that the Siren can't be fought with weapons — only with meaning.
must_include: each player gets a named moment
chapters.tone: Determination through action. Fighting apathy with meaning.

chapters.title: The Shadow Over Driftmere
chapters.scaffold: A shadow falls across Driftmere — not at night, but in the middle of the day. The Leviathan's Shadow blots out the sun, and the sea churns gray beneath it. The Emberbearers must give their all this week. This is the culmination of Arc 2. If they succeed, the shadow burns away, and something vast shifts in the deep — not gone, but retreating. The Chronicler appears again, writing: 'The coast holds. But the Hollow is patient. The Emberbearers must grow faster.' A new path opens: into the Ironspine Mountains.
must_include: the shadow blotting out the sun
transition hint to Arc 3: Ironspine Depths
each player gets a heroic named moment
chapters.tone: Epic conclusion to the coastal arc. Triumph with a shadow of what's to come.

chapters.title: The Iron Gate
chapters.scaffold: A desperate message arrives from the Forgekin: the Ember Veins beneath the Ironspine Mountains are being poisoned. The Hearthstone dims as the mountain's pulse weakens. The Emberbearers travel to the gates of the mountain range, where they meet Rustvein — an ancient Forgekin automaton corrupted by Hollow energy. It guards the mine entrance with grinding, rusted limbs, attacking anything that approaches. The Chronicler appears, scratching warnings into the stone: 'The deeper you go, the louder the Hollow sings.'
must_include: Rustvein description as a corrupted automaton
chapters.tone: Industrial dread. The warmth of the mountain is failing, and something metallic waits in the dark.

chapters.title: Rust and Resolve
chapters.scaffold: The Emberbearers push deeper into the mines, chipping away at Rustvein's corruption with every task. Active households see the Hollow energy drain from the automaton's frame; quieter weeks see Rustvein's joints grinding louder. Professor Ignis arrives with mining records and historical data about the Forgekin, emphasizing that knowledge is as important as strength in the depths.
must_include: each player gets a named moment
chapters.tone: Gritty determination. The clang of metal and the glow of Embershards in the dark.

chapters.title: The Distortion
chapters.scaffold: Deeper in the mountain, the Emberbearers find a cavern that shouldn't exist. The Echoing Hollow twists every sound: encouragement becomes mockery, plans become confusion, laughter becomes weeping. It's not a creature — it's a wound in the world, a place where the Hollow has whispered so long that reality itself has bent. The only way through is with clear minds and steady hearts. Educational challenges become the primary weapon this week.
must_include: the cavern described as a sentient space
chapters.tone: Psychological unease. The enemy is not a monster — it is a place that has forgotten what truth sounds like.

chapters.title: The Betrayer's Lament
chapters.scaffold: Slag was once a Forgekin — a proud craftsman of the Ember Veins. He invited the Hollow in, believing its power would make him stronger, faster, better. Now he is half-metal, half-ash, filled with regret he cannot express. He attacks not out of malice but out of a broken heart that doesn't know how to stop. The Emberbearers' steady effort reminds him of the warmth he lost. Every chore and lesson rekindles a small flame in his chest.
must_include: the small flame in his chest
chapters.tone: Tragic empathy. The saddest enemies are the ones who were once like you.

chapters.title: The Heart of the Mountain
chapters.scaffold: At the deepest point of the Ember Veins, the Emberbearers find the source of the corruption: the Veinworm, a massive parasitic creature burrowed into the living Emberlight of the mountain, drinking it dry. The mountain trembles as it feeds. The Emberbearers must channel everything they have into their Embershards over this final week of the arc. If they succeed, the surge of pure Emberlight is more than the Veinworm can stomach. It releases its grip and flees into the dark. The Chronicler appears, writing in golden light: 'The Ember Veins pulse strong again. But the Hollow King grows impatient. The Emberbearers must not rest long.' A new path opens: across the Dustmere Plains.
must_include: the Veinworm description as a massive parasite
transition hint to Arc 4: Dustmere Plains
each player gets a heroic named moment
chapters.tone: Epic conclusion to the mountain arc. The earth itself was wounded, and the Emberbearers healed it.
